title: Heatwave warning in nine Telangana districts on May 25 - The Hindu
full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave warning in nine Telangana districts for Monday.
According to IMD's latest bulletin, heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets in Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Khammam, Nalgonda, Suryapet, Mahabubabad, Warangal and Hanamkonda districts.
Additionally, warm nights are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets in Adilabad, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Khammam and Nalgonda districts.
The bulletin also stated that thunderstorms accompanied with lightning and gusty winds are very likely to occur at isolated places in Vikarabad, Sangareddy, Mahabubnagar, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Narayanpet and Jogulamba Gadwal districts.
Hyderabad and its surrounding areas for the next 24 hours will see a partly cloudy sky. “Light rain or thundershowers accompanied by gusty winds are very likely to occur in parts of the city towards evening or night. The maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 39C and 27C respectively,” the bulletin said.
Published - May 24, 2026 06:54 pm IST

title: 16 people die amid heatwave in Telangana as temperatures cross 45℃ | People are advised to stay in from 11 am to 4 pm | Inshorts - Inshorts
full_text:
16 people die amid heatwave in Telangana as temperatures cross 45℃
At least 16 people have died amid intense heat across seven districts in Telangana during the current summer season. In response, the state government has announced an ex-gratia of ₹4 lakh each to the families of the deceased. Temperatures in places like Khammam, Ramagundam, and Adilabad have crossed 45℃, with IMD issuing alerts for heatwave conditions and warm nights.
